parameters.jsCode:
return [{ json: {
  feedIds: [122, 156, 159],
  skipPattern: '/opinion|op-ed|editorial|/commentary|/voices|personal essay',
  dedupThreshold: 0.5,
  snippetWords: 40,
  sections: ['week', 'month'],
  maxItems: { week: 6, month: 6 },
  maxWordsPerBullet: 22,
  systemPrompt: 'You write ultra-concise dashboard digests as JSON.',
  userPromptTemplate: 'Build a "Politics" digest as JSON from LGBTQ+ news articles. Cover two kinds of stories, mixed together in each section: new or advancing legislation, court rulings, executive actions, and policy changes affecting LGBTQ+ people (prioritize dangerous or restrictive measures, say which state or body is acting), and genuinely positive stories -- protections passing, bans struck down, firsts, community support. "week": at most {{MAX_WEEK}} bullets from the past 7 days. "month": at most {{MAX_MONTH}} bullets from 8-30 days ago -- a separate, older set of articles listed below, so there is no overlap with "week" by construction. The limits are maximums, not targets: if there is little or no real news, return fewer bullets or an empty array -- never pad with weak items. Summarize aggressively: group articles covering the same story, bill, or theme into ONE bullet spanning all of them -- do not write one bullet per article. Each bullet is ONE plain sentence, {{MAX_WORDS}} words max, rewritten in your own words -- never copy article text, no dates, no markers. Exclude opinion pieces, editorials, personal essays, and advice columns entirely. Skip celebrity gossip.\n\nPast 7 days:\n{{WEEK_ENTRIES}}\n\n8-30 days ago:\n{{MONTH_ENTRIES}}'
} }];

const cfg = $('Config').first().json;
return cfg.feedIds.map(feedId => ({ json: { feedId } }));

const s = $getWorkflowStaticData('global');
return [{ json: s.digest ?? { week: [], month: [], updated: null } }];
